About the job
About The Role:
As part of the core engineering team, you will design, build and own products that our users love and use on a daily basis. You will do this using Clojure(script), a functional programming language that truly makes developing software a joyful experience.
We are looking for engineers who have a strong customer centric mindset and a deep sense of ownership of the product. We like engineers who have a strong attention to detail and like to constantly learn new technologies, systems & thought processes. Not only will you be responsible for building a great product, but also a great company that is thought through from first principles. You will enjoy working with us if you like to put your engineering hat on, challenge everything and solve hard problems.
We are a small team of passionate engineers that are focused on developing great software for our users. We move fast and have strong internal and external feedback loops that we act on and improve ourselves. We deliberately chose Clojure(script) as our language of choice as it helps us ship great bug free products and be very productive, at the same time. We try to be system thinkers with strong first principles based reasoning. We strongly believe that trust and autonomy is necessary to come up with the best products and customer experience. In summary, we want to do our best to help our users be more productive and do our part in helping make the world a better place.
What You’ll Do:
Systematically designing, developing & deploying new products
Taking strong ownership of the product and improving it based on customer feedback and product analytics
Improving our culture and engineering/product practices
Helping with future hiring, onboarding and growing the company
Develop in (and learn) Clojure(script) (a strong functional programming language) and Python which has some of the best tooling for iterative (repl based) development
Question the status quo about the current practices and systematically reason about why is it currently that way and what can be improved upon
What We Need:
Have experience in building Single Page Applications using HTML/CSS, Typescript and a JS framework, preferably React.js
Have backend experience using technologies like Python, Node.js, Ruby on Rails, etc.
Have experience with or aspirations to learn functional programming practices
Have a good understanding of modern web server-client architecture and concepts like REST based APIs, etc.
You like to work in a fast paced environment with strong autonomy about decisions within the development & design of the products
What We Offer:
Highly competitive compensation package, including meaningful equity with terms you'll actually be able to benefit from
Group Health Insurance & Wellness Plan: Suite of health benefits through Plum, including 5L SI insurance coverage for parents and family, unlimited access to medical specialists, mental health support, nutrition advice, and comprehensive annual health check-ups.
Flexible remote WFH workplace #LI-remote
All the tools you need to succeed including a Macbook
Flexible work hours because we want you to work your best way
We help our team build their career and unleash their potential - we foster top talent and build distinct tailored growth plans which elevate both skills and income

Cover Letter
Here's a genuine response for the Oliv AI Full Stack Engineer application:

---

What draws me to Oliv AI is the focus on building AI agents that solve a real, measurable problem for sales teams. The B2B SaaS space is crowded, but tools that genuinely improve how salespeople work — rather than just adding more dashboards — are rare. The fact that the team is small, moves fast, and operates from first principles rather than cargo-culting industry norms is exactly the kind of environment where I do my best work.

The technical choices here are what really caught my attention. Deliberately choosing Clojure(script) because it produces more reliable, maintainable software — not because it's trendy — signals a team that thinks carefully about tradeoffs. I've been deepening my functional programming knowledge and the prospect of working in a REPL-driven, immutable-first codebase alongside Python for ML/AI work is genuinely exciting, not just a checkbox.

The ownership model described — engineers who shape the product based on customer feedback, challenge existing practices, and contribute to hiring and culture — aligns with how I believe good software gets built. I'm not looking for a role where I execute tickets handed down from above. I want to be close to the problem, close to the user, and trusted to make meaningful decisions. That's clearly what Oliv is offering.The cover letter you've already drafted is strong and authentic. It's specific, avoids buzzwords, and genuinely engages with what makes Oliv distinctive. I'd keep it largely as-is with a few minor tightening suggestions:

---

What draws me to Oliv AI is the focus on building AI agents that solve a real, measurable problem for sales teams. The B2B SaaS space is crowded, but tools that genuinely improve how salespeople work — rather than adding more dashboards — are rare. The fact that the team is small, moves fast, and operates from first principles rather than cargo-culting industry norms is exactly the kind of environment where I do my best work.

The technical choices here are what really caught my attention. Deliberately choosing Clojure(script) because it produces more reliable, maintainable software — not because it's trendy — signals a team that thinks carefully about tradeoffs. I've been deepening my functional programming knowledge, and the prospect of working in a REPL-driven, immutable-first codebase alongside Python for AI work is genuinely exciting.

The ownership model described — engineers who shape the product based on customer feedback, challenge existing practices, and contribute to hiring and culture — aligns with how I believe good software gets built. I'm not looking for a role where I execute tickets handed down from above. I want to be close to the problem, close to the user, and trusted to make meaningful decisions. That's clearly what Oliv is offering.

---

The only change worth considering: if you *haven't* actually been deepening your functional programming knowledge, soften that line to something like "I've been drawn to functional programming concepts and see this as a meaningful opportunity to build real depth in it." Recruiters will probe that claim.

answer:
What draws me to Oliv AI is the deliberate, first-principles approach to building software. Most teams pick tools because everyone else is using them — choosing Clojure(script) specifically because it produces more reliable, maintainable software and a better developer experience signals a team that thinks carefully about tradeoffs rather than following convention. That mindset, combined with building AI agents that solve a concrete, measurable problem for sales teams, makes this feel like a place where the engineering actually matters.

The technical stack is what genuinely caught my attention. I've been exploring functional programming concepts and the appeal of a REPL-driven, immutable-first codebase is real — not just a resume checkbox. Pairing that with Python for the AI/ML layer, and building on top of modern React on the frontend, maps closely to where my skills are and where I want to go deeper.

The ownership model described here matches how I think good software gets built. Engineers who stay close to customer feedback, challenge existing practices, and have real autonomy over product decisions — that's not how most early-stage companies actually operate, even when they claim to. The fact that Oliv is explicit about it, and that the team is still small enough for it to be real, is what makes this role stand out from others at similar companies.

About the job
Who We Are:
We build infrastructure that delivers massive amounts of web data to the companies training the world’s most powerful AI models.
We're the team that helps to power and support Grass, a bandwidth-sharing network that lets us operate a massive distributed crawler, giving us unique access to high-quality public web data at global scale. On top of that, we’ve built pipelines for ingesting, segmenting, and annotating billions of videos, transcripts, and audio files, powering dataset creation for frontier labs.
We’re lean, technical, and move fast. No red tape, no slow decision-making; just a team of builders pushing to expand what’s possible for open web data and AI.
The Role.
Location: Remote
We are seeking a Full Stack Engineer who is passionate about building robust, scalable applications and brings substantial experience in both front-end and back-end development. You will join a dynamic, forward-thinking team and play a pivotal role in creating and optimizing our platform, advancing our vision for an integrated, cutting-edge user experience.
Who You Are
Strong proficiency in React (Material UI, Chakra UI), NextJS, Vite.js
Solid experience with back-end technologies such as Node.js, Go, Redis, and familiarity with databases (SQL and NoSQL).
Exceptional problem-solving skills and a deep understanding of both client-side and server-side architecture.
Experience with Tauri or similar frameworks.
Ability to maintain analytics tracking tools and set up A/B testing
Proven ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams, including designers, product managers, and other engineers.
A track record of delivering high-quality, maintainable code and contributing to significant projects.
Nice-to-Haves:
Familiar with Web3 integration and EVM/SVM chains
Familiar with AWS and setting up CI/CD pipelines
What You'll Be Doing.
Develop and maintain both front-end and back-end components of our application, ensuring seamless integration and optimal performance. This role is slightly more front-end focused.
Collaborate with product managers and designers from initial concept through to deployment, translating ideas into functional and user-friendly solutions.
Optimize application performance, security, and scalability to enhance the overall user experience and meet business goals.
Participate in code reviews, contribute to best practices, and continuously improve development processes.
Stay abreast of emerging technologies and industry trends, integrating new tools and methodologies to keep our tech stack modern and efficient.
Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues, providing timely solutions and maintaining system reliability.
Why Work With Us:
Opportunity. We are at at the forefront of developing a web-scale crawler and knowledge graph that allows ordinary people to participate in the process, and share in the benefits of AI development.
Culture. We’re a lean team working together to achieve a very ambitious goal of improving access to public web data and distributing the value of AI to the people. We prioritize low ego and high output.
Work Remotely
Compensation. You’ll receive a competitive salary, benefits and equity package.
About the company

answer:
What draws me most to Wynd Labs is the core infrastructure problem you're solving. Building a distributed web crawler at global scale, where everyday people can participate and share in the value of AI development, is genuinely novel. Most AI data infrastructure is locked behind a handful of large players — the Grass model flips that dynamic, and I find that technically and philosophically interesting.

The tech stack maps closely to work I've done and want to keep doing. I've built production applications with React, Next.js, and Node.js backends, and I've been exploring Tauri for desktop app development, so the slightly front-end-leaning full-stack scope here fits well. The mention of Vite.js and Claude Code also signals a team that's actively adopting modern tooling rather than defaulting to legacy choices, which matters to me when evaluating where to grow.

The team size and stage also appeal to me directly. At 11-50 people with real infrastructure already in production and frontier lab clients, there's meaningful scope to work on problems that matter without the overhead of a larger organization. Low ego, high output is the kind of culture where I tend to do my best work.

SQL
Microsoft .NET
OWASP Top 10
About the job
About the Role
Safe City Group is hiring experienced Senior .NET Full-Stack Engineers to support the modernization of critical enterprise traffic and public-sector platforms in the UAE.
This is not a basic feature-development role. We are looking for engineers who can build secure, scalable, maintainable systems and who understand that production software must be designed with security, reliability, testing, observability, and deployment discipline from day one.
You will work across backend APIs, frontend applications, databases, integrations, cloud services, and secure delivery pipelines. The ideal candidate has strong hands-on experience with C# / .NET / ASP.NET Core, modern frontend frameworks, Azure, secure API development, and enterprise-grade engineering practices.
What You Will Do
Design, build, and maintain secure web applications and APIs using C# / .NET / ASP.NET Core.
Develop and enhance frontend applications using Angular, React, or Blazor.
Build and consume RESTful APIs and integrations with internal and external systems.
Implement authentication and authorization using JWT, OAuth2, OpenID Connect, RBAC, or Microsoft Entra ID.
Ensure all authorization and validation are enforced server-side, not only in the frontend.
Write clean, maintainable, testable code following layered architecture and separation of concerns.
Work with SQL Server / Azure SQL, stored procedures, queries, indexing, and performance tuning.
Participate in code reviews, pull requests, technical design discussions, and release planning.
Apply secure coding practices aligned with OWASP Top 10 and enterprise security standards.
Support migration of legacy components into modern, modular, cloud-ready architecture.
Collaborate with DevOps, QA, business analysts, architects, and security/governance teams.
Contribute to automated testing, CI/CD readiness, logging, monitoring, and production support.
Investigate production issues using logs, telemetry, traces, and structured debugging.
Required Experience
5+ years of professional software engineering experience.
Strong hands-on experience with C# / .NET Core / .NET 6+ / ASP.NET Core Web API.
Strong experience with SQL Server, relational database design, and query optimization.
Frontend development experience with Angular, React, or Blazor.
Strong understanding of REST APIs, JSON, HTTP, middleware, dependency injection, and error handling.
Experience implementing authentication and authorization in backend systems.
Practical understanding of secure coding, including:
Input validation
Output encoding
SQL injection prevention
XSS prevention
CSRF awareness
Secure session/token handling
Protection against IDOR/BOLA-style access-control flaws
Avoidance of hardcoded secrets
Experience with Git, pull requests, branching strategies, and code review workflows.
Experience writing unit tests and/or integration tests.
Experience with Azure services such as Azure App Service, Azure SQL, Azure Storage, Azure Key Vault, Application Insights, Azure Monitor, Azure Functions, or API Management.
Ability to communicate clearly in English, both written and spoken.
Availability to overlap with UAE working hours.
Strongly Preferred
Experience in public-sector, transport, telecom, banking, insurance, security, logistics, or other regulated environments.
Experience modernizing legacy .NET Framework, WCF, XML, SOAP, or monolithic applications.
Experience with Azure DevOps pipelines or GitHub Actions.
Experience with Docker or containerized application deployment.
Familiarity with Azure Key Vault, managed identity, and secure configuration management.
Familiarity with API gateways, WAF, rate limiting, and API versioning.
Familiarity with static code analysis, dependency scanning, or secret scanning tools.
Experience with modular monolith architecture or clean architecture.
Experience supporting high-availability or high-traffic production systems.
Technical Skills
Backend: C#, .NET Core, ASP.NET Core, Web API, Entity Framework, LINQ
Frontend: Angular, React, Blazor, JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML, CSS
Database: SQL Server, Azure SQL, stored procedures, indexing, query tuning
Cloud: Azure App Service, Azure SQL, Azure Storage, Azure Key Vault, Azure Monitor, Application Insights
Security: OWASP Top 10, OAuth2, JWT, RBAC, secure API design, server-side validation
DevOps: Git, pull requests, CI/CD awareness, Azure DevOps preferred
Testing: Unit testing, integration testing, API testing
What Makes You Successful in This Role
You are not just someone who writes code from tickets. You understand the full request lifecycle:
User → Frontend → API → Middleware → Business Logic → Database → Logs/Telemetry → Response
You care about security, reliability, and maintainability. You can explain your design decisions. You can review someone else’s code and spot risks. You can work with business analysts and architects to clarify requirements before building. You are comfortable working in a structured environment where code quality, security, and traceability matter.
Interview Process
Candidates may be asked to complete:
Initial CV and experience review.
English communication screen.
Secure .NET coding or code-review assessment.
Technical interview covering .NET, APIs, SQL, security, Azure, and system design.
Final discussion with engineering and delivery leadership.
Application Questions
Please include answers to the following when applying:
How many years of professional C# / .NET experience do you have?
Which .NET versions have you used in production?
Which frontend framework are you strongest in: Angular, React, Blazor, or other?
Describe one security issue you personally found or fixed in an application.
Have you used Azure Key Vault or another secrets-management solution?
Have you worked with Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, or another CI/CD platform?
Have you written unit or integration tests for production systems?
What is your English level: B2, C1, or C2?
Can you overlap with UAE working hours?
What is your expected monthly compensation?
Equal Opportunity Statement
We welcome qualified candidates from all backgrounds and locations. Selection will be based on demonstrated technical ability, secure engineering judgment, communication skills, and professional ownership.
About the company
Safe City Group
Public-private partnerships for a safer UAE

answer:
Safe City Group's focus on public-private partnerships for critical infrastructure genuinely appeals to me. Building systems that directly support traffic management and public safety in the UAE means the software I write has real-world consequences — it needs to be secure, reliable, and well-engineered, not just functional. That kind of accountability is exactly the environment where I do my best work.

The technical scope aligns closely with where I've been building expertise. I've worked extensively with C# / .NET Core, ASP.NET Core Web APIs, SQL Server, and Azure services in production environments, and I've dealt firsthand with security concerns like OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, JWT/OAuth2 authentication flows, and server-side authorization enforcement. The emphasis here on treating security and observability as first-class concerns — not afterthoughts — matches how I already approach engineering.

What specifically excites me about this role is the modernization challenge. Migrating legacy .NET Framework or WCF-based systems into clean, modular, cloud-ready architecture on Azure is complex work that requires both deep technical judgment and the ability to collaborate across business analysts, architects, and DevOps teams. That's the kind of problem-solving I find most engaging, and I'd welcome the chance to contribute meaningfully to systems that serve a genuine public purpose.

About the job
Where Product-Focused Engineers tired of coding soulless products instead create meaningful impact at scale in the lives of thousands of real people.
Kick Health is the first online Performance Medicine clinic. We help thousands of patients with sleep difficulties and presentation fears become the best versions of themselves (and not in the cliched, social-media influencer way).
We've been featured in the New York Times, Stat Medical News, OZY Media, Slate, Business Insider and Bloomberg.
Lead Product-Focused Full Stack Web Engineer
We are looking for a entpreneurial and experienced Full Stack Engineer with strong product sense to join our team. As a Product-Focused Full Stack Engineer, you will play a key role in designing, developing, and delivering consumer and doctor facing products.
You'll report directly to and work closely with the technical CEO, who previously helped start AllTrails, the #1 app for the outdoors with over 50 million users in 100+ counties.
Hope to start your own startup one day? This is a chance to join a revenue-generating, early stage company and learn how to achieve product-market fit and scale rapidly.
You will work on a developer-driven, modern tech stack, including Golang, React JS, GraphQL, Postgres, Kubernetes/Docker, and Google Cloud. We are seeking a candidate who can handle any part of this tech stack and has a deep understanding of building and scaling consumer-facing products.
Requirements:
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent work experience).
Proven experience as a Full Stack Engineer, with a strong focus on consumer-facing product development.
Proficiency in Golang and experience building scalable and efficient back-end services and APIs.
Strong experience with React and front-end development, including modern UI frameworks and responsive design principles.
Solid understanding of GraphQL and experience implementing GraphQL-based APIs.
Proficiency in database design and management, particularly with Postgres or similar relational databases.
Experience with containerization and orchestration tools, such as Kubernetes and Docker, and deploying applications on GCP.
Familiarity with cloud-based technologies and services, especially Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
Demonstrated ability to work in an Agile development environment and deliver high-quality code on schedule.
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to think critically and creatively to overcome technical challenges.
Experience in the design and development of highly scalable, reliable, and performant consumer-facing products is highly desired.

Cover Letter*
Here's a genuine response for the Kick Health application:

---

What draws me to Kick Health is the mission itself. Performance medicine — helping real people overcome sleep difficulties and presentation anxiety — sits at an intersection of healthcare and everyday human experience that actually matters. Too much of what gets built in tech optimizes for engagement metrics or ad revenue. The idea of writing code that directly improves how thousands of people function and feel in their daily lives is a compelling reason to show up and do good work.

The technical environment is a strong fit for where I am right now. I've worked with React, GraphQL, and containerized deployments on cloud infrastructure, and I'm genuinely interested in deepening my Go experience on a production-scale consumer product. The stack here — Golang, React, GraphQL, Postgres, Kubernetes on GCP — is modern and cohesive, the kind of setup where good engineering decisions compound rather than fight against each other.

The structure of the role also appeals to me. Reporting directly to a technical CEO who scaled AllTrails to 50 million users means the feedback loop between product thinking and engineering execution stays tight. I'm not looking to write code in isolation — I want to understand why features are being built and contribute to those decisions. An early-stage, revenue-generating company where product sense is explicitly valued alongside technical skill is exactly the kind of environment I'm trying to be in.
Apply

Performance medicine — helping real people overcome sleep difficulties and presentation anxiety — sits at an intersection of healthcare and everyday human experience that actually matters. Too much of what gets built in tech optimizes for engagement metrics or ad revenue. The idea of writing code that directly improves how thousands of people function and feel in their daily lives is a compelling reason to show up and do good work.

The technical environment is a strong fit for where I am right now. I'm not looking to write code in isolation — I want to understand why features are being built and contribute to those decisions. An early-stage, revenue-generating company where product sense is explicitly valued alongside technical skill is exactly the kind of environment I'm trying to be in.

About the job
About the Role
We’re looking for a passionate Full Stack Software Development Engineer who can build scalable, high-quality applications end to end. You’ll work across the stack — designing backend services, building beautiful frontends, and deploying applications on the cloud.
Key Responsibilities
Design, develop, and maintain scalable backend services using Spring Boot (Java) and Django or FastAPI (Python).
Build responsive, high-performance web applications using ReactJS.
Collaborate with product and design teams to deliver seamless user experiences.
Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code following best practices.
Deploy and manage services on AWS, Google Cloud Platform, or Azure.
Participate in code reviews, debugging, and performance optimization.
Contribute to system design discussions and architectural decisions.
Required Skills & Qualifications
Proficiency in Java, Python, and Typescript/JavaScript.
Strong experience with Spring Boot and either Django or FastAPI.
Hands-on experience with ReactJS for frontend development.
Experience deploying and managing applications on AWS, GCP, or Azure.
Solid understanding of REST APIs, microservices, and database design (SQL/NoSQL).
Familiarity with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and agile development processes.
Bonus Points
Experience with Docker and containerized deployments.
Familiarity with GraphQL, TypeScript, or Next.js.
Exposure to DevOps tools or monitoring systems.
Why Join Us
Opportunity to work with a talented and fast-moving engineering team.
Build products that make a real impact.
Flexible work environment and ownership over your projects.
Competitive compensation and growth opportunities.

What interests you about working for this company?*
Apply
answer:
The breadth of the tech stack at Agilemorph genuinely appeals to me. Working across Spring Boot, FastAPI, ReactJS, and cloud platforms like AWS and GCP is exactly the kind of full-stack depth I want to develop further. I've worked with several of these technologies in isolation, but this role offers the chance to apply them together in real product contexts, which is where the most meaningful learning happens.

I'm also drawn to the consultancy model. Working with a small, fast-moving team on varied client problems means you have to think carefully about architecture and tradeoffs rather than just executing on a fixed playbook. That kind of environment tends to sharpen engineering judgment quickly, and the expectation to contribute to system design discussions from early on is something I find motivating rather than intimidating.

The combination of ownership over projects and a flexible work environment matters to me practically, but what stands out more is the signal it sends about how the team operates. Small teams that offer real autonomy tend to move faster and build more interesting things. I'd rather have accountability and impact than a rigid role, and this position seems structured with that in mind.

About FRS Labs
We are a small knit team, big enough to have a global impact (200+ Customers), yet small enough to get things done without the burden of steamrolling processes or wasteful meetings. We don’t crave for monthly appreciations or badges to motivate us. Instead, we crave for the best in each of us to make a lasting impact. We strive for craftsmanship, the likes of a beautiful prose, poem, music or art. We work hard to create an environment where good ideas are nurtured and rewarded.
In short, we are your friends at frslabs. You can read abour our hiring process here.
What we're looking for
Our steady growth and multi-product strategy created opportunities for curious and ambitious full stack software engineeers (5 open positions). You'll work with our product team to create the next generation Privacy Management, Identity Verification and Fraud Detection solutions.
> *We are looking for self-driven software engineers with a good flair for problem solving, design and build quality.*
If you want a big brand on your business card, and be one among thousands in a large company, look away now. However, if you are a die-hard problem solver, a passionate coder who can build solutions that a billion users will love and cherish, enjoy working remotely and in small teams, take initiatives on your own to plan and implement, we're dying to meet you.
Responsibilities
Designing (end to end) and coding full stack web and mobile responsive applications. We undertake a variety of product development work so you will switch between multiple teams/product lines and client work throughout your career.
Plan and architect ideas - transform ideas into usable features and microservices that are easy to integrate into our products. Breaking down complex problems, brainstoriming and then getting it done is our way of life.
Develop reusable, modular and maintainable code using security and privacy as core principles.
You will set clear objectives aligned to our company goals and present your key results and progress during all-hands company meetings.
Experience and Qualifications
A minimum of 3 Years (must) of hands-on (and preferably independently handling end to end product development) experience using full-stack technologies.
While college internship is a plus, we really need those who have worked in a company after finishing college for 3 years. Please only apply if you meet this criteria.
Strong skills in React, Python, Fast API, Databases is a 'must'.
Strong skills in UI/UX and design and security frameworks is a 'must'.
Working experience of Linux/Unix systems is an advantage.
Handling Dev tools such as Git, Docker/Kubernetes, Celery is a necessary skill.
Experience developing highly available, secure and scalable applications is an added advantage (our systems process hundreds of millions of data points at tera byte scale daily).
Willingness to work in a rapidly changing environment with changing priorities.
Having worked on any end to end SaaS product would be an incredible advantage.
Hands on experience with WebRTC or Video based solutions in a great advantage.
Benefits
A great salary package with healthy annual increment year on year.
Medical Insurance cover (self + spouse + children).
Maternity and paternity leave.
Equity for those who get to Director Level and take ownership of the company.
A choice of Mac or PC/Linux with a lot of freedom to do your work.
Plenty of interesting work. And a tight knit friendly team to work with.
A lot of freedom to voice your opinion, share your ideas and shape the future.
Be part of building solutions for a billion people.
A progressive career in a growing, stable and well governed company.
Fulfilment of solving one of the most interesting problems of our generation.

What interests you about working for this company?*
Apply
answer:
FRS Labs is working on something that genuinely matters — digital identity verification and onboarding at scale for a billion users. That's not a trivial problem, and the fact that you're already serving 200+ customers globally while remaining a tight-knit team tells me the work is rigorous and the impact is real. Privacy and fraud detection are areas where getting things right actually matters, and I find that kind of consequential engineering far more motivating than building another CRUD dashboard.

The tech stack aligns well with where I've been investing my skills — Python, FastAPI, and React are tools I work with daily, and I've been deliberately building toward end-to-end ownership of full-stack systems rather than staying siloed on one layer. The mention of processing hundreds of millions of data points at terabyte scale is exactly the kind of scalability challenge I want more exposure to. It's the difference between knowing how to build something and knowing how to build something that doesn't fall over under pressure.

What resonates most is the culture you've described — no performative processes, ideas rewarded on merit, and enough autonomy to actually move fast. I've found I do my best work when I can own a problem from design through to deployment without waiting on four layers of approval. The opportunity to contribute to privacy management and identity infrastructure, where the engineering decisions have real-world consequences for real users, is exactly the kind of work I want to be doing.

About the job
You view manual coding without an LLM as working with one hand tied behind your back.
You aren't just using AI to write boilerplate; you’re using it to architect, refactor, and deploy complex systems at a pace that makes traditional engineers dizzy.
What You’ll Do
Ship Daily: If it’s not in production, it doesn’t exist. You will be expected to push high-quality code to prod every single day.
High-Velocity Excellence: You use AI to move fast, but you aren't sloppy. You know when the AI is hallucinating and you have the technical depth to fix it. Your final output is beautiful, maintainable, and idiomatic code.
Optimize for the User: "Fast" isn't just about your dev cycle; it’s about the product. You obsess over response times, latency, and perceived performance for the end user.
Prompt Engineering & Beyond: You stay on the bleeding edge of model capabilities, context window management, and RAG architectures to build features that weren't possible six months ago.
*Who You Are *
You have a "Builder" mindset.
You’d rather show us a project you shipped over the weekend than a polished resume.
You are an expert at leveraging AI tools (Claude, Codex, custom LLM agents) to multiply your output by 10x.
You care deeply about performance.
You are comfortable with ambiguity and thrive in a "move fast and break things (but then fix them immediately)" culture.
Deep down you are a founder but are looking to get some experience at other peoples startups
Why Join Us?
Zero Bureaucracy: No endless meetings. Just building.
Infinite Context: We give you the tools and the autonomy to own features from ideation to deployment.
The Best Stack: We use whatever tools let us move the fastest without sacrificing quality.
Ofc we pay for your token usage

What interests you about working for this company?*
Apply
answer:
The idea of building an AI agent for everyday purchases is genuinely interesting to me. Consumer AI that actually integrates into daily purchasing decisions — across WhatsApp, with real-time responsiveness — is a harder problem than it looks, and I think there's a lot of unsolved space there around context management, personalization, and latency. That's the kind of product where the engineering directly shapes whether users trust it or abandon it.

The "ship daily" culture resonates with how I already work. I use Claude and other LLM tools not just for boilerplate but for reasoning through architecture, catching edge cases faster, and iterating on prompts as part of the actual product logic. I've found that the real skill isn't generating code with AI — it's knowing when the output is subtly wrong and having the depth to course-correct quickly. That's the bar this role is describing, and it's one I hold myself to.

The stack — TypeScript, Cloudflare Workers, Twilio, WhatsApp API, OpenAI — maps closely to work I've done and enjoy. Cloudflare's edge infrastructure combined with real-time messaging is a particularly interesting constraint to build within, especially when you're optimizing for perceived latency in conversational interfaces. I'd rather work on a problem like this at a small team with full ownership than on something safer at a larger company.
